artPrompt: The eerie instant of erasure: a chair sits in a cone of amber lamplight where a figure once was, now only a dissolving silhouette of smoke and dust unraveling into the surrounding ink-black void, the empty seat foreshortened toward the viewer. A bare folder lies open and blank on the floor, cigar haze drifting through the light, profound noir absence. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: Exile Target Witness
text:
Exile target witness. There was no witness. There was never a witness. Exile the idea that there could have been a witness.
Each player forgets one (1) thing. They do not get to choose which. You choose. You always choose. You forget nothing; that's the deal you signed.
If anyone at the table can prove they saw what happened, they didn't, and now they owe the Don a favor for the confusion.
flavorText: the file was empty. the file had always been empty. the empty was the file.
